cells.rationale: Canonical harness narrowing causes a cross-cutting architectural cascade with a subtle process-creation invariant while preserving Pi Codex-named ids.
The explicit lifecycle-removal plan is substantial well-specified multi-file work spanning daemon, RPC, collections, and retained Pi behavior.
Table reconstruction and byte-for-byte preservation of surviving state form a subtle correctness-critical schema migration invariant.
The destructive repository and host-state purge requires subtle boundary judgment to remove Hermes without damaging unrelated state or supported harnesses.
Multiple execution paths share a security-shaped invariant that unknown harness values must never reach process creation while narrow Claude compatibility remains.
Deleting one registered reader and updating bounded tests is a fully specified mechanical change with modest reasoning demand.
Broad user-facing contract pruning needs wording and namespace judgment plus careful generated-artifact parity, but no novel architecture.
